title: Teijsmanniodendron coriaceum
taxonomy: Teijsmanniodendron coriaceum is a species of plant in the family Dipterocarpaceae. It belongs to the order Malvales and the clade Rosid. This classification places it within the larger group of flowering plants known as angiosperms. The plant is also commonly referred to by its family name, which is derived from the Greek words 'dipteros,' meaning two-winged, and 'karpos,' meaning fruit. Teijsmanniodendron coriaceum is a tropical tree native to Southeast Asia.
parts: I couldn't find any information on the medicinal parts of the plant Teijsmanniodendron coriaceum.
intro: Teijsmanniodendron coriaceum, also known as the "Malaysian ironwood," is a species of plant in the family Annonaceae. This plant is prized for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Teijsmanniodendron coriaceum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, this plant is commonly used to prepare various herbal remedies, including teas and infusions, as well as extracts and tinctures, to harness its therapeutic benefits.
